data.title: AXO Copper Corp. increases previously announced bought deal to $35 million.
data.description: In a recent report, it has been revealed that the global carbon dioxide levels have reached a new record high of 416 parts per million (ppm) in May 2021. This increase marks a significant milestone as it surpasses the pre-industrial levels by over 50%. The data also shows that the last time Earth had similar CO2 levels was around 4 million years ago, during the Pliocene epoch. Scientists warn that this unprecedented rise in CO2 levels is a critical concern as it contributes to the warming of the planet and exacerbates climate change. The need for urgent action to reduce greenhouse gas emissions is emphasized to prevent further environmental damage and its associated consequences.

data.title: SIPA awards exemplary projects using SIP construction in residential & commercial buildings in 2026.
data.description: A prominent trade association in the construction industry has recently acknowledged exceptional residential and light commercial projects utilizing Structural Insulated Panel (SIP) construction to provide an outstanding building envelope. The Structural Insulated Panel Association (SIPA) revealed the recipients of the prestigious 2026 Building Awards. This recognition highlights the innovative and superior construction techniques employed in these projects. The awards serve as a testament to the growing popularity and effectiveness of SIP construction in creating durable and energy-efficient buildings.

data.title: Gold hits new highs; Bitcoin at $88,918.51, Ethereum at $2,998.87, Dogecoin at $0.1241. $233M liquidated in last 24 hours. Fed keeps rates steady. BTC may hit $95,000. Discovery stalled at $90,000. Trading slightly down. Volume drops for ETH. Market remains uncertain.
data.description: On Wednesday, leading cryptocurrencies and stocks traded flat, with gold continuing its record-breaking rally as Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell stated that rate cuts are not currently a primary scenario. Bitcoin saw a -0.44% change, priced at $88,918.51, while Ethereum dropped -0.50% to $2,998.87. XRP decreased -0.24% to $1.89, Solana fell -1.89% to $124.65, and Dogecoin was down -0.87% at $0.1241. Bitcoin attempted to break above $90,000 but faced resistance, falling back to $88,000, while Ethereum briefly crossed $3,000 but couldn't maintain it. Trading volume for Ethereum dropped 21% in the past 24 hours. Crypto-related stocks such as Strategy Inc. (NASDAQ: MSTR) and Coinbase Global Inc. (NASDAQ: COIN) saw declines of 1.94% and 0.66% respectively. Approximately $233 million was liquidated from the market in the past 24 hours, with equal amounts of bullish longs and bearish shorts being wiped out. Bitcoin's open interest and spot price both fell by 0.49%. Retail and whale accounts on Binance showed a mix of holdings.

data.title: New labor-for-hire business Couple More Guys® opens in Lake Country, offering two-person crews for small jobs.
data.description: Based in Lake Country, DELAFIELD, Wisconsin, as of January 28, 2026, the family-owned labor-for-hire business Couple More Guys® has launched its booking calendar. The company provides insured, uniformed two-person crews for tasks that do not necessitate a contractor. The services cater to in-between jobs and are now available for booking.

data.title: Toshiba in Japan introduces gate driver for high-current automotive motors, offering samples for enhanced safety and reduced wiring.
data.description: In Kawasaki, Japan, Toshiba is providing samples of a new compact gate driver with SPI compatibility, designed to improve safety and minimize wiring in high-current automotive motor systems. This innovative product aims to streamline operations and enhance efficiency in the automotive industry.

data.title: Sony increases investment in Soneium to $13 million, in collaboration with Startale, post mainnet success, for long-term plan.
data.description: Sony has increased its investment in Startale by $13 million, indicating a shift towards a more long-term infrastructure approach. This move comes about a year after Soneium's mainnet was launched.

data.title: Investor alert: Hagens Berman investigating F5 (FFIV) for undetected hack, nation state infiltration. Feb 17th deadline.
data.description: Reed Kathrein, a partner at a law firm, is examining allegations of top-notch security measures amid reports of stolen source code. Investors in F5, Inc. (NASDAQ: FFIV) have until Feb. 17 to respond to this notice by shareholder rights law firm Hagens Berman. This scrutiny comes after claims were made about the company's industry-leading security practices.

data.title: Venu Holding Corporation cancels $75 million public stock offering, no shares sold. #VENU #NYSEA.
data.description: Venu Holding Corporation, a company specializing in upscale live music venues and premium hospitality destinations, based in Colorado Springs, has decided to cancel its planned public offering of $75,000,000 worth of common stock. The announcement came on January 28, 2026, following the initial announcement of the offering on January 27, 2026. Consequently, no shares of the Company's Common Stock will be sold as part of this offering.

data.title: LG Energy Solution reported KRW 23.7 trillion revenue, KRW 1.3 trillion operating profit in 2025. Aiming for revenue increase. Expanding ESS, EV battery products, and entry into new applications. Operating profit margin was 5.7%.
data.description: LG Energy Solution reported its financial results for 2025, with a consolidated revenue of KRW 23.7 trillion and an operating profit of KRW 1.3 trillion. Despite a decrease in revenue from the previous year, there was a significant increase in operating profit. The company aims for a mid-teen to 20% increase in annual revenue in 2026, targeting more than 90GWh in new orders for ESS batteries and expanding the production capacity to over 60GWh, primarily in North America. LG Energy Solution plans to introduce new EV battery products and expand into new applications like robotics. They have optimized asset management by reallocating capacity, establishing production sites in Europe, and enhancing capital efficiency through asset sales. The company successfully diversified its production and customer bases in 2025 and sees a positive outlook for the future.
